title: edit-plan.json — the approvable, executable edit plan (videos/<project>/work/edit-plan.json)
description: The centerpiece. Built by the planner from the script, the VO alignment (script-map.json), the asset catalog (assets.json), and the editing brain (brain/EDITING-BRAIN.md). The owner approves it scene-by-scene or wholesale; approval freezes a revision (append-only). The execution compiler reads the approved revision and emits timeline.json (scene.schema.json shots), an sfx-plan.json, and a music mix invocation. A human-readable mirror, edit-plan.md, is regenerated on every write because make-tsx already reads edit-plan.md as its source of truth.

mode.description: vo_script = the drop-folder mode (script + VO narration, no talking head); talking_head = the sibling mode that already has clean-cut/make-tsx. This schema serves both; in talking_head mode `master` is the recorded cut and vo_span refers to edited-transcript spans.

rev.description: Revision number. Bumped on every FROZEN approval. Revisions are append-only: rev N is written to work/plan-revs/edit-plan.rev-N.json before rev N+1 is generated. Mirrors the versions philosophy.

parent_rev.description: The rev this one was derived from (owner edited rev 2 -> produced rev 3). null for the first plan.

status.description: draft=planner still writing; in_review=presented at the approval gate, owner acting per scene; approved=frozen, compiler may run; superseded=a newer rev was frozen.

brain_ref.description: The editing-brain rulebook the planner consumed (pacing, coverage, treatment-selection, sound taste). Authored by a separate expert; the planner READS it, it is not defined here.

sources.description: The upstream artifacts this plan was built from, for provenance + staleness checks (if an input's sha changes, the plan is stale).

script.description: sha256 of the resolved script file

vo_primary_sha.description: sha256 of the VO take chosen as the master narration

master.description: How the bake master is constructed. In vo_script mode there is NO talking-head video, so the master is SYNTHESIZED (VO audio + a base video track) BEFORE bake so bake.py's contract (mux master audio, pass-through master video in the gaps) holds unchanged. See PIPELINE-DESIGN.md section on the VO-mode master.

kind.description: vo_synth = compiler builds master.mp4 = [VO audio] + [base video track]; recorded_cut = talking_head mode, the clean-cut master.

audio.description: vo_synth: the picked/assembled VO track (the primary narration). recorded_cut: the master's own audio (this field then just records identity).

base_track.description: vo_synth only: the video that shows through wherever no cutaway covers the timeline (bake pass-through segments read master video here). Keeps bake.py unmodified — the master is a normal A/V file.

kind.description: brand_slate = branded holding card (BrandBg/BrandProof-style, rendered once); primary_broll = a chosen wide/calm clip looped/held; color = flat brand color; ambient_loop = a slow-motion texture. Default per brain: brand_slate. This is the ONLY thing the owner sees in un-covered gaps, so treatment aims to cover most of the timeline.

asset_sha.description: if kind=primary_broll: the source asset's sha256 from assets.json

fps.description: comp fps (30 for preview per the kit)

duration_s.description: total VO/master length; equals the last scene's vo_span end

music.description: Video-level music bed intent. The compiler turns this into a tools/mix_music.py invocation over the SFX'd bake. Per-scene section intent lives in each scene.sound.music_section.

bed_id.description: id from media/library/music/catalog.json (e.g. ambient-pad, tech-pulse). null = no bed.

duck_db.description: extra dB the bed drops under the VO (mix_music.py --duck). Sidechain keyed by the VO+SFX bus.

scenes.description: The ordered plan. One scene per script beat (a beat may be a paragraph or a sentence group, per the aligner + brain). Scene order == render order.

warnings.description: Planner-surfaced issues the owner should see at the gate (coverage gaps, low-confidence alignment, VO ad-libs off-script). Non-blocking; informational.

id.description: Stable scene identity, carried verbatim into timeline.json scenes[].id and into remotion comp names (PascalCase when a Remotion beat).

order.description: Explicit render order; ties broken by array position.

text.description: The script beat's prose, verbatim. This is what the owner reads at the approval gate and what the matcher tokenizes against asset tags.

script_ref.description: Where in the script this beat came from (for round-trip edits).

title.description: Short human label for the scene card at the gate, e.g. 'Hook: the 4am problem'.

vo_span.description: Where this beat lands on the VO/master timeline, from script-map.json. The scene's on-timeline duration = end_ms - start_ms; cutaways for this scene are placed inside this span.

align_confidence.description: fuzzy-alignment score of beat->transcript. Low => an 'alignment_low_confidence' warning and a nudgeable span at the gate.

vo_take_sha.description: which VO take this span is measured against (supports per-section takes / retakes).

intent.description: The beat's narrative job, inferred from position + script cues + brain rules. Drives treatment and sound taste (a hook and a cta are sounded differently).

coverage.description: Ranked candidate assets for this beat, best first. Produced by the matcher: score = token overlap (beat.text tokens vs asset filename/folder/tags) + duration fit (asset dur vs vo_span dur) + class fit (does the beat want a clip/image/screencast?). The chosen one is referenced by treatment; the rest are alternates the owner can swap to at the gate (they become candidate takes downstream).

reasons.description: Legible match reasons for the gate, e.g. ['tag:dashboard matches beat', 'dur 6.2s fits 5.8s span', 'class:broll fits demo'].

chosen.description: true for the single asset the treatment currently uses.

treatment.description: HOW this beat is realized on screen. Exactly one `kind`. A GAP (kind=generate) is FIRST-CLASS: a scene with no matching asset gets kind=generate + a prefilled prompt derived from beat.text, and stays status=planned until a take exists. The compiler wires generate treatments to the existing provider/courier system (work/inbox + jobs), it does not invent generation.

kind.description: broll_cutaway=a camera clip replaces the base video for the span; image_kenburns=a still via the kit's ImageRevealShot (slow scale/opacity, the existing 'ken burns'); screencast=a screen-capture clip or a fake-screencast TSX; remotion_beat=an authored kit shot (statement/diagram/UI clone) built by make-tsx; generate=GAP, hand off to Remotion/Hyperframe courier with `prompt`; base_only=no cutaway, the base track carries the beat (used for pure-narration transitions).

engine.description: Which engine produces the take. media=an ingested clip/image (broll_cutaway/image_kenburns/screencast); remotion=an authored/kit beat; fable|hyperframe=generation providers for GAPS. Carried into scene.schema.json `engine`.

asset_sha.description: For media treatments: the chosen asset (== the coverage entry with chosen:true). null for generate (until fulfilled) and remotion_beat (comp, not asset).

comp.description: For remotion_beat: the Remotion composition id (e.g. ImageRevealShot, LevelTitleShot, or a to-be-authored shot). make-tsx builds it; the compiler records the id.

props.description: For remotion_beat: values for the comp's propsSchema (passed to bake via scene.props). e.g. {title, subtitle, color, src}.

kenburns.description: For image_kenburns: the move. Maps onto ImageRevealShot / a pan-zoom.

prompt.description: For generate GAPS: the prefilled generation prompt, DERIVED from beat.text + intent + brand, editable at the gate. This is what the provider/courier `spec.prompt` receives. Example seed: '<beat.text> — cinematic b-roll, <brand look>, 6s, no text'.

provider_hint.description: For generate: which courier to prefer ('scene 5 needs something -> Remotion or Hyperframe or both'). 'either' lets the owner pick at the gate; 'both' is expressed by requesting candidates from each.

duration_s.description: Intended on-screen duration; defaults to the vo_span length. The compiler sets fit policy if the take's real duration differs.

fit.description: Carried into scene.schema.json `fit`: what to do if the take duration != span duration.

sound.description: The beat's sound intent. The compiler splits this into (a) sfx-plan.json events (per the existing suggest-sfx grammar) and (b) music-section shaping. NOT a mixer here — it declares intent that the existing mix_sfx.py / mix_music.py execute.

music_section.description: How the bed should behave under this beat (breath=let it come up in a gap; silence=duck to nothing). Advisory to the final music mix; the video-level bed is one continuous track ducked by VO.

sfx.description: SFX cues for this beat, in the suggest-sfx vocabulary. The compiler rebases `at` (scene-relative or word-anchored) to absolute master `at_s` and emits sfx-plan.json events. Library-first: sfx_id must resolve in media/library/sfx/catalog.json (or become a palette recipe).

function.description: The suggest-sfx function-first grammar: motion=whoosh, tension=riser, emphasis=impact/pop, snap=click. The brain/suggest-sfx picks the concrete sfx_id.

sfx_id.description: Concrete library id (e.g. whoosh-soft, pop-reveal). Optional at plan time; resolved at compile per library-first.

anchor.description: Where the cue lands. Either a word (preferred, from script-map/transcript) or a scene-relative offset or a treatment moment.

word.description: the narration word to hit (a pop on 'boom')

on.description: the treatment moment (cut-in whoosh, reveal pop)

gain_db.description: relative to the clip's normalized level; brain/short-form calibration applies. Optional; suggest-sfx defaults fill in.

layer.description: part of a build-and-drop hero moment (riser->impact). Only the 2-3 biggest beats.

optional.description: deniable texture; carried to sfx-plan event.optional.

duck.description: duck SFX/music under the VO for this beat.

pacing.description: Pacing notes for this beat, consumed by the brain-guided compiler when deciding cut density and hold vs. cut of the cutaway inside the span.

energy.description: brain-inferred; hooks/payoffs high, explains medium, breaths low.

min_hold_ms.description: shortest a single cutaway should stay up (avoid strobing).

status.description: planned=treatment chosen but no take yet (all GAPS start here; also media scenes before conform); ready=a take exists / comp authored, executable; approved=owner confirmed this scene at the gate; blocked=would break the bake (missing asset, unfulfilled GAP at execute time). Maps onto scene.schema.json status {planned,generating,draft,approved} at compile (approved plan scenes with a take -> draft; GAP still open -> planned).

approval.description: Per-scene approval record for the gate (owner approves/edits per scene or wholesale).

edited_fields.description: which fields the owner changed from the planner's proposal (e.g. ['treatment.asset_sha','vo_span.end_ms']).
